features.summary: Costco reported Q3 fiscal 2022 net income of $1.353 billion ($3.04 per share), up from $1.22 billion ($2.75 per share) in the prior year. Net sales increased 16.3% to $51.61 billion with comparable sales up 14.9% globally. Membership fee income grew 9.2% to $984 million, with renewal rates hitting all-time highs at 92.3% in US/Canada and 90% worldwide. The company opened one net new warehouse in Q3 and expects 24 net new locations for the full fiscal year.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued inflationary pressures with Q3 price inflation estimated at 7%, up from 6% in Q2 and 4.5-5% in Q1. The company plans to open 10 additional warehouses in Q4 for a total of 27 new locations (24 net) in fiscal 2022. CapEx is estimated at just under $4 billion for the year. Management indicated they are not planning a membership fee increase at this time despite approaching the typical 5.5-year cycle, citing the current macro environment and inflation burden on members.
features.risks: Key risks include ongoing supply chain disruptions, higher commodity prices, increased transportation costs, and labor cost pressures from new wage agreements. China operations faced significant headwinds with the Shanghai location closed for six weeks due to lockdowns, resulting in $35 million in lost sales. LIFO charges of $130 million in Q3 reflect continued inventory cost inflation, and management expects additional LIFO impacts if inflation persists.
features.key_drivers: Strong gasoline sales drove significant traffic with gallons up in the high teens to low 20s versus industry growth of 1-2%. Fresh food margins faced pressure from difficult year-over-year comparisons and higher raw material costs. E-commerce grew 7.9% on top of 38% growth in the prior year, with continued expansion in grocery delivery and big-and-bulky categories. Executive membership penetration reached 43% of the member base, representing over 71% of worldwide sales.
